blurb: Slim upright fingers wrapped in neat radial stars of coppery-gold spine, clustering into a colony. The uncrested cousin of the Brain Cactus already on the shelf — same species, ordinary growing point.

care.light: Bright light with plenty of direct sun keeps the fingers stout and well-spined; too little and they grow pale and stretch.
care.water: Soak-and-dry on the sparing side — thorough drink, then bone dry before the next. Nearly dry through winter.
care.soil: A gritty cactus mix that drains almost instantly.
care.temperature: Room temperature; keep above ~35°F. A cool, dry winter rest sets up spring flowers.
care.dormancy: A summer grower that rests cool and dry in winter — that dry rest triggers the ring of blooms.
care.feeding: A dilute low-nitrogen cactus feed once or twice in summer.
care.potting: Repot infrequently with folded newspaper or gloves; it clusters, so divide offsets when it fills the pot.
care.propagation: Twist off a finger, let it callus, and set on dry mix to root.
care.bloom: A crown-ring of small cream-to-yellow flowers after a cool dry winter.
care.troubles: Overwatering rot, plus mealybugs and red spider mites.
care.toxicity: Non-toxic — but the fine spines catch skin, so handle with care.
